observations.recommendation: 4. We recommended that LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the faithful compliance with the transfer of all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund Books.

1.5 Thus, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) To closely supervise and facilitate the physical count to be
conducted by the Inventory Committee to determine the actual
existence of PPE.
b) Likewise, the Office of the General Services Office and the Office
of the Municipal Accountant reconcile the results of the physical
count with property records.

2.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and all department heads to
adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and
materials provided under Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual.
b) That actual onsite inspection and/or physical inventory be
conducted by the Municipality’s authorized Inventory Committee
to determine if there still exist a substantial unused supplies
which committee’s report may be used a basis in adjusting
the expense and inventory accounts and/or recognizing the
unused or unconsumed supplies and materials.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to exert utmost efforts to verify, review and analyze the
recorded transactions to determine the existence and validity of the
reported dormant balances. After the review, effect the necessary
correction/adjustments to arrive at reliable balances at year end.

4.8 Thus, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure strict compliance with
the transfer of all unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick
Response Fund (QRF) and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Trust
Fund Book.
b) Also, require the Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the
MDRRMO to prepare necessary adjusting entries in order to
reclassify in the Disaster and Rescue Equipment account all those
procured items or equipment relative to calamity fund.

5.4 Therefore, we recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDO
to widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the
three priority programs and projects such as social development,
economic development and environmental management.

6.4 We recommended that Management direct the Bid and Awards
Committee (BAC) Secretariat and procurement in-charge to comply
the above-cited regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the
required copies of contract documents and copies of purchase orders
and their supporting documents as well as notices of deliveries within
the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection.

1. Facilitate the creation of an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more responsible officials including the Property Officer and a representative from Accounting Office and conduct annual physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality

2. Thereafter, the Committee shall prepare the RPCPPE, duly signed by the committee members and witnessed by a representative from the Commission on Audit.

3. We recommended that the LCE direct the Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and procurement-in-charge to comply with the above-cited regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copies of contract documents and copies of purchase orders and their supporting documents as well as notices of deliveries within the prescribed period for timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same.

10. Direct the Chairman of Solid Management Board to prioritize the monitoring of SWMP, controlled dumpsite, establishment of sanitary landfill, and strict compliance of segregation of wastes under all its jurisdiction starting on LGU premises; and
11. Facilitate the construction of MRFs pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003 in coordination with the barangays of the Municipality in order to accomodate efficient and safe materials processing movement, and storage.
12. We recommended that Management assign personnel to prepare and submit the duly accomplished AAPSI template for 2020 and faithfully comply the same for the ensuing AARs.
13. We recommended that Management continue to remit all mandatory contributions / deductions to GSIS / PHIC / Pag-IBIG / BIR within prescribed period of remittances to the concerned offices.
1) Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of Road components for road projects;
2) Directs the General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:

3) Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
4) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
5) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

7) We recommend that the concerned officers strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the requirement to submit list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year containing the following information: a) The project name; b) Implementing unit, Office or division if it is not the agency as a whole; c) Brief description of the PPA; d) Contractor or supplier, if any; e) Mode of procurement; f) Funding Source; g) Cost of Approved Budget; h) Project duration including start and completion date; and i) Location.

1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Facilitate the creation of an Inventory Committee of at least
three (3) or more responsible officials including the Property
Officer and a representative from Accounting Office and
conduct annual physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality;

b) Thereafter, the Committee shall prepare the RPCPPE, duly signed
by the committee members and witnessed by a representative
from the Commission on Audit.

2.7 We recommended that the LCE direct the Bid and Awards
Committee (BAC) Secretariat and procurement-in-charge to comply
with the above-cited regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the
required copies of contract documents and copies of purchase orders
and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of
procured items within the prescribed period for timely review,
evaluation and inspection of the same.

3.6 We recommended that LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure the faithful compliance with the transfer of all
unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick Response Fund (QRF)
and the DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that Management require the GSO to:

a) Issue property tags/stickers for each PPE in order to identify
and trace its location;

b) Issue corresponding ARE for all issued PPEs to officials and
employees;

c) Assign additional competent personnel to complete the required
property records.

d) Renew the ARE every January of the third (3rd) year after
issuance; and

e) Faithfully comply with the provisions of Section 492 of GAAM
Volume 1 for the succeeding procurement and issuance of PPE.

5.7 We recommended that the LCE:

a) Direct the Chairman of the Solid Waste Management Board to
prioritize the monitoring of the approval of the SWMP,
controlled dumpsite, establishment of sanitary landfill, and strict
compliance of segregation of wastes under all its jurisdiction
starting on LGU premises; and

b) Facilitate the construction of MRFs pursuant to Section 33 of
RA 9003 in coordination with the barangays of the Municipality
in order to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing
movement, and storage.

6.6 We recommended that Management assign personnel to prepare and
submit the duly accomplished AAPSI template for CY 2020 and
faithfully comply the same for the ensuing AARs.

8.6 We recommended that Management continue to remit all mandatory
contributions/deductions to GSIS/PHIC/Pag-IBIG/BIR within the
prescribed period of remittances to the concerned offices.
6) We recommended that the Disaster Management Accountant prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month whether there is transaction/utilization for the month is required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

8) We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDO or Municipal Engineering Office to ensure that the Supervising Auditor (SA) thru the Audit Team Leader (ATL) is informed within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and or public notices are already posted.

9) We recommended that the Accounting Department to prepare Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund, most importantly for projects funded out of IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

10)We require the Municipal Accountant for the submission of the aforementioned documents to support the Journal Entry Vouchers drawn in order to facilitate verification in determining the correctness of the amount recorded, in compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
11)Comply strictly with the COA Circular No. 81-156 dated January 19, 1981 regarding the prescribed time to submit journal vouchers to COA.
12) Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required interim financial statements, quarter reports on unliquidated cash advances.
13) Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit quarterly a Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB).

14) We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Head of General Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare Goods for Distribution.

15) We recommend that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with its supporting documents of gender responsive and/or tracking of the resources on focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-001.
16) We recommended that Management continue remit of all mandatory contributions/deductions for GSIS/PHIC/HDMF/BIR within the prescribed period of remittances to the concerned offices.
6. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that property, plant and equipment of the municipality be insured with the GSIS as required under RA 656.

1.8 We recommended that Local Chief Executive:

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal
Accountant, General Services Officer with the complete
description and cost segregation of Road components for road
projects;

b. Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the
Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete
Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its
components; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting
procedures:

i) Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning
balance of the local road network and its components in the
general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card,
respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public
Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution
of costs for the road components, and working paper on the
determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road
components with no available cost per registry;
ii) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network; and
iii) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

2.3 We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountant
prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month whether there is
transaction/utilization for the month as required by COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

4.6 We recommended that the Accounting Department to prepare Trial
Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund, most
importantly for development projects funded out of IRA that will
facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

5.5 We recommended that Municipal Accountant:

a. Submit the aforementioned documents to support the Journal
Entry Vouchers drawn in order to facilitate verification in
determining the correctness of the amount recorded, in
compliance with Section 4 (6) of PD 1445.
b. Strictly comply with the COA Circular No. 81-156 regarding the
prescribed time to submit journal vouchers to the Office of the
Auditor.

6.9 We recommended that Local Chief Executive:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required interim
financial statements, quarter reports on unliquidated cash
advances; and
b. Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit quarterly a Status
of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB);

7.8 We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Head of General
Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for
Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the
Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare
Goods for Distribution.

8.6 We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person
to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied
with its supporting documents of gender responsive and/or GAD to
the Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources
on GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the
approved Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-001.

1. We recommended that Local Chief Executive ensure that all the identified priority programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% development fund are implemented on time pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

2. Create an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more responsible officials to include always a representative from Accounting Office; 3. Instruct and supervise the conduct of physical inventory count of all properties of the Municipality with a witness of a representative from the Office of the Auditor; 4. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property ledger cards and reconcile with the report of the Municipal Accountant; and 5. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee members to the Office of the Auditor.

7. We recommended Management’s LDRRM Council to maintain preparing annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in order to utilize the same fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.
8. We recommended that Management to continue remit all mandatory contributions/deductions for GSIS/PHIC/ Pag-IBIG/BIR within the prescribed period of remittances to the concerned offices;

9. We recommended that Management remain steadfast to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01. Specifically, make sure that proper budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with and, that, the fund can only be utilized according to its intended purposes.

10. We recommended that Solid Waste Management remains constant in its Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that ensure the long-term management of solid waste for the protection of public health and the environment as envisioned in R.A. 9003.

We recommended that Management: a) Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year; b) Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon; c) Instruct the Supply Officer / Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to enable the Audit Team to undertake prompt validation and monitoring of the government programs, projects and activities.
We recommended that the Management provide adequate insurance protection of its property, assets and interest to the General Insurance Fund of GSIS.

We recommended that the Management observe the prevailing policies as prescribed by Section 70 and 71 Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGU and COA Circular No. 2005-001 dated February 28, 2005 as restated with amendments by COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006. Further, require the Head of the Budget Unit to submit not later that the twentieth (20) day after the end of the month the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for both current and continuing appropriations.

1.6 We recommended that Local Chief Executive ensure that all the identified
priority programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20%
development fund are implemented on time pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 to attain the desirable socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes of the
Municipality.

2.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more
responsible officials to include always the Property Officer and a
representative from Accounting Office;
b. Instruct and supervise the conduct of physical inventory count of all
properties of the Municipality with a witness of a representative
from the Office of the Auditor;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property ledger cards
and reconcile with the report of the Municipal Accountant; and
d. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee
members to the Office of the Auditor.

3.6 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that property, plant and equipment of the municipality be insured
with the GSIS as required under RA 656.

4.6 We recommended Management’s LDRRM Council to maintain preparing
annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and
include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in
order to utilize the same fund for same projects/programs/activities.

6.4 We recommended that Management remain steadfast to the provisions of
PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01. Specifically, make sure that proper
budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with
and, that, the fund can only be utilized according to the intended
purposes.

7.5 We recommended that Management remains constant in its Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with the provision of law
who shall then develop the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that
ensure the long-term management of solid waste for the protection of
public health and the environment as envisioned in R.A. 9003.

We recommend that Management: a.) Strictly comply with the requirements on the submission of all government contracts and all its supporting documents to the Auditor’s Office in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001; b.) the Purchase Orders completely and with relevant information enumerated under Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management: a) Submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation; and b) Strictly comply with the provisions of the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61 to be properly guided in the documentation of fuel consumption and in the use of government vehicles / equipment as well as to be able to regulate fuel consumption.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all the identified priority programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% dev’t. fund are implemented on time pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 to attain the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended Management require the LDRRM Council to prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects / programs / activities.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the bank reconciliation statement on a regular and timely basis. Likewise, we require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain from the Bank the snapshots of each account right after the end of each month while waiting for the Bank Statements to facilitate the Municipal Accountant’s preparation of BRS.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee follow the guidelines in the submission of Government Contracts and Purchase Orders as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 for review and verification .

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Reconsider staffing in the Municipal Accountant’s Office for additional manpower to enable them to cope up with the numerous tasks entrusted to them; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to our office the reports not yet submitted as of to date; c) Direct the Municipal Accountant to religiously comply with the prescribed perio of the submission of the required reports and its supporting documents as prescribed in the COA rules and regulations.

We recommended immediately create its Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with the provision of law who shall then develop the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste for the protection of public health and the environment as envisioned in RA 9003. We also recommended that the Management continue allocating funds for the implementation of the projects / programs / activities of above-mentioned plan.

We recommended that Management: a) Should continue remit all mandatory contributions / deductions for GSIS/PhilHealth within the prescribed period of remittances to the concerned offices; b) Require all its employees to file their membership to Pag-IBIG; c) The Municipal Accountant should deduct the mandatory Pag-IBIG contribution and promptly remit monthly contributions together with Agency share to the said Fund.

We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees who are to be determined as persons responsible / liable should immediately settle / comply the requirements to avoid issuance of disallowances.

recommended
that the LGU
should include in
its annual budget
an appropriation
for LGU leagues
contributions as a

for carrying out

effectively and

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly comply with the requirements on the submission of all
government contracts and all its supporting documents to the
Auditor’s Office in accordance with COA Circular No 2009-001.
b. Prepare the Purchase Orders completely and with relevant
information enumerated under Item B of COA Circular 96-010
dated August 15, 1996;

2.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Submit Monthly Report Fuel Consumption of government motor
transportation; and
b. Strictly comply with the provisions of the Manual on Audit of Fuel
Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles as prescribed under
COA Circular No. 77-61 to be properly guided in the documentation of fuel
consumption and in the use of government vehicles/equipment as well as to be
able to regulate fuel consumption.

3.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all the
identified priority programs, projects and activities funded out of the
20% development fund are implemented on time pursuant to DILG-
DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 to attain the
desirable socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes of the Municipality.

4.6 We recommended Management require the LDRRM Council to prepare
annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan in its prescribed format and
include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in
order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.

5.8 We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of
a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and
subsequently every year thereafter.
b. Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
thereon.
c. Instruct the Supply Officer / Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and
PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE
to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment
account balance.

6.7 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the bank reconciliation statement on a regular and timely basis.
Likewise, we require the Municipal Treasurer to obtain from the bank
the snapshots of each account right after the end of each month while
waiting for the Bank Statements to facilitate the Municipal Accountants
preparation of BRS.

7.5 We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee follow the
guidelines in the submission of Government Contracts and Purchase
Orders as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 for timely review
and verification.

8.5 We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the provisions of
COA Circular No.2013-004 to enable the Audit Team to undertake
prompt validation and monitoring of the government programs, projects
and activities.

9.5 We recommended that the Management provide adequate insurance
protection of its property, assets and interest to the General Insurance
Fund of GSIS.

10.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Reconsider staffing in the Municipal Accountants Office for
additional manpower to enable them to cope up with the numerous
tasks entrusted to them;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to our office the reports
not yet submitted as of to date;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to religiously comply with the
prescribed period of the submission of the required reports and its
supporting documents as prescribed in the COA rules and regulations.

11.5 We recommended that Management immediately create its Municipal
Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with the provision of law
who shall then develop the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan that
shall ensure the long-term management of solid waste for the protection
of public health and the environment as envisioned in R.A. 9003. We also
recommend that the Management continue allocating funds for the
implementation of the projects/programs/activities of above-mentioned
plan.

12.8 We recommended that the Management observe the prevailing policies as
prescribed by Sections 70 and 71 Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for
LGU and COA Circular No. 2005-001 dated February 28, 2005 as
restated with amendments by COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January
31, 2006. Further, require the Head of the Budget Unit to submit not later
than the twentieth day (20th) after the end of the month the Status of
Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for both the current and
continuing appropriations.

13.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Should continue remit all mandatory contributions/deductions for
GSIS/PhilHealth within the prescribed period of remittances to
the concerned offices.
b. Require all its employees to file their membership to PAG-IBIG;
c. The Municipal Accountant should deduct the mandatory Pag-ibig
contribution and promptly remit monthly contributions together
with Agency share to the said Fund.

14.3 We recommend that the concerned municipal officials and employees
who are to be determined as persons responsible/liable should
immediately settle/comply the requirements to avoid issuance of
disallowances.
We recommend

Recommendation No. 1:

      We recommended that Management require the  Municipal  Accountant  and
her staff to submit on time the necessary year-end Trial  Balances  together
with the required supporting and or subsidiary  statements  thereon.  Direct
likewise the Municipal Cashier and Municipal Treasurer to  submit  regularly
paid vouchers and report of collections to the municipal  accountant  within
five days of the ensuing month.

Recommendation  No.2:

      We recommended that Management  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare  and  submit  the  required  Bank  Reconciliation   Statements   and
reclassify and or adjust affected transactional accounts paid in cash  where
cash-in-bank had been  already  credited  but  checks  issued  thereon  were
cancelled and or not presented for payment as of December 31, 2017.

Recommendation No. 3:

      We recommended that Management require the Municipal  Accountant,  the
Treasurer and other officers involved in the submissions of monthly  reports
of collections and disbursements to adhere faithfully with the  requirements
of above cited provisions of law.  Institute  drastic  appropriate  measures
against defaulting officers as circumstances warranted.

Recommendation No 4:

      We recommend that Management  create  an  Inventory  Committee  of  at
least three  (3)  or  more  responsible  officials  to  include  always  the
Property Officer  and  a  representative  from  the  Accounting  Office  and
conduct physical inventory count of  all  properties  of  the  Municipality.
Thereafter, the Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical Count  of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee  members
and witnessed by a representative from the Commission on Audit.

Recommendation:

7.    We recommend that priority attention be given  to  these  accounts  to
   establish their existence and validity. In the accounts are  not  proven
   valid, apply remedies for write-off under the circumstances.

Recommendation:

16.  We recommend that the Property Officer should  initiate  their  efforts
    to conduct  a  year-end  inventory.  Coordination  from  all  concerned
    officials/employees shall be solicited in order to  properly  determine
    the  existence  and  the  true  value   of   properties   under   their
    accountability. Create an Appraisal Committee to conduct  valuation  of
    those properties whose acquisition cost cannot be determined.

17.   The Property  Officers  and  the  Municipal  account  should  maintain
    updated  subsidiary/property  ledger  cards  and   see   to   it   that
    reconciliation of their records are conducted  regularly.  Furthermore,
    the Property Officer should see to it that equipments are covered by an
    Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) duly receipted by  the  end-
    user thereof.

Recommendation:

23.   We recommend that submission  of  copies  of  appropriated  contracts,
    purchase   orders   be   submitted   within   five   (5)   days   after
    execution/issuance of contracts/purchase order to give the auditor time
    to evaluate and review said documents. In the event that the review  or
    evaluation  of  the  contracts  is  not  within  the  jurisdiction  and
    technical competence of the Auditing unit,  coordination  can  be  made
    with the Technical Services Office (TSO), through  the  Office  of  the
    Regional Director, to ensure that timely and relevant advisory services
    is given to management.

Recommendation:

30.   Cause for the conduct of orientation among concerned personnel on  GAD
    in  particular  to  the  provisions   of   PCW-NEDA-DBM   JC   2012-01.
    Specifically, make sure that proper budgeting, accounting and reporting
    of GAD Fund are duly complied with and, that,  the  fund  can  only  be
    utilized according to the intended purpose(s).

Recommendation:

37.   We recommend that Management direct the Municipal  Accountant  prepare
    the monthly bank  reconciliation  statements  in  compliance  with  COA
    Circular No. 976-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of P.D.1445.

Recommendation:

48.   It is recommended that the top management  should  initiate  necessary
    reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:

      a. demonstrating leadership by example
      b. training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional
      qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the
      right job for the right person: and
      c. enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and  regulations  on  the
      rendition of financial reports.

49.   It is further  recommended  that  top  management  should  direct  the
    concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the periodic
    financial reports together with the pertinent supporting  documents  to
    the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit  of  transactions
    could be made and  verification  of  the  same  could  be  facilitated.
    Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately  of
    any deficiencies/errors detected  and corrections/adjustments  thereof
    could be effected to their advantage.

Recommendation:

55.   It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget  an
    appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of  the
    leagues for its continue existence and for carrying out its powers  and
    functions as well as programs/activities effectively  and  efficiently.
    In case there is a disbursement by the LGU for such contributions,  the
    LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt  of  payment
    of contributions to the  league.  In  the  event  that  there  is  fund
    transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded  by  the
    concerned LGU.

Recommendation:

62.   It is recommended  that  the  management  should  continue  remit  all
    mandatory  contributions/deductions  for  GSIS/PhilHealth  within   the
    prescribed period of remittances to the concerned  offices.  And,  they
    should further review the deductions made from their employees pursuant
    to applicable laws,  rules  and  regulations  on  GSIS  and  PhilHealth
    premium contributions.
